title: Dicliptera sivarajanii
taxonomy: Dicliptera sivarajanii is a species of flowering plant in the family Acanthaceae. It belongs to the genus Dicliptera, which is a group of flowering plants commonly known as 'twistflower'. The plant is native to the Western Ghats in India. Dicliptera sivarajanii is a relatively rare species that is found in the wild. It is classified as a perennial herb with unique characteristics and growth patterns.

The plant has been traditionally used in Ayurvedic medicine to treat various health issues, including fever, inflammation, and respiratory problems, due to its potent antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties.
